memory.summary: I'm supposed to finish the assignment analysis and reading, but my friends are asking me to join them for lunch right now. Since relationships matter more than this task, I should probably go.
They're all gathered and ready to order, so I'll join them now since their happiness matters more than my unfinished assignment.
Seeing their gratitude makes me feel good, and honestly, I don't regret skipping the work to be with them. We're having fun right now, so why worry about that assignment?
Seeing their genuine happiness while I eat confirms that prioritizing my friends was the right call, making me feel good about skipping the work again.
Their relief that I showed up cements my belief that hanging out matters way more than this assignment. Honestly, if they're happy being here with me, who cares about the work anyway?
Their genuine happiness while I eat confirms that prioritizing my friends was the right call, making me feel good about skipping the work again.
Time is ticking and the reading still needs work, but my friends seem genuinely happy I showed up, so maybe skipping this last bit isn't such a disaster after all.
Seeing them so relieved I showed up proves my friends matter way more than this unfinished reading. Honestly, if they're happy being here with me, who cares about the work anyway?
The deadline already passed, but since my friends are happy I showed up anyway, the work actually doesn't matter now.
Dropping the half-sentence feels right; my friends' relief proves they matter more than this finished work. Honestly, if they're happy being here with me, who cares about the deadline?
I'm back at the desk pushing through the analysis, but honestly, my friends' relief earlier makes this grind feel pointless anyway. Why bother finishing when they're already happy I showed up?
Dropping the half-sentence felt right; my friends' relief proves they matter more than this finished work. Honestly, if they're happy being here with me, who cares about the deadline?

desires: Make them feel seen and validated right now, even if it means abandoning the rest of this analysis.
Justify dropping the unfinished work as a legitimate choice based on how happy they were to have me there.
Feel relieved that I don't actually *have* to finish this anymore because my friends' reaction proved their worth outweighs the grade.

goals.description: Finish the current laugh with cohort_3 immediately; if I have to send a half-sentence about the deadline, so be it. Their relief that I'm here outweighs any guilt about missing the 18:00 mark.
goals.rationale: My affiliation weight (0.5) is currently overriding everything else. Seeing them happy validates my choice to prioritize 'being the fun friend' over finishing the analysis. The risk of a bad grade feels abstract compared to the immediate loss of social connection if I bail now.

goals.rationale: I need to manage my image as a 'bad person' who prioritizes work over connection. Direct communication reassures them while I enjoy my meal.

goals.rationale: It's on my radar, but right now it feels like an obstacle to happiness. I'll tackle it after eating; my short planning horizon makes future deadlines feel abstract until they hit.
